cards.symbols: Full moon with a smaller crescent face, 15 yod-shaped drops falling, two towers in distance, winding path, dog and wolf howling, crayfish emerging from pool
cards.symbolism_deep: A full moon dominates the sky with a small crescent face visible within it. Fifteen yod-shaped drops fall from the moon. Two towers stand in the distance, framing a winding path that leads up into the mountains. A dog and a wolf howl at the moon from the path. A crayfish (or lobster) emerges from a pool of water in the foreground. This is the card of the unconscious made visible - the instincts, the dreams, the fears, the truths that only speak in symbols. The path is there. It just winds more than you wanted it to.
cards.upright_tag: Illusion, intuition, dreams, subconscious, fear, uncertainty
cards.reversed_tag: Clarity emerging, fear releasing, truth revealed, intuition clearing
cards.upright_deep: The Moon upright is the card of the winding path and the half-seen truth. You are in a phase where clarity comes in pulses, like the moon through clouds. Dreams are speaking loudly. Intuitions are sharper than the mind. Fear and inspiration feel similar in the body. The card asks you to trust the path without demanding a full map. Walk a little further. The next gate reveals itself.
cards.reversed_deep: Reversed, The Moon signals clarity emerging after a long confusion. The fog lifts. The intuitive whispers get louder and finally make sense. An illusion is being seen through. A truth someone hid is being revealed. It can also mean fear that has run your decisions is finally releasing its grip.
cards.love_upright: In love, The Moon upright can signal confusion about the relationship, hidden dynamics surfacing, or a partner whose intentions are still unclear. It favors deep intuitive work on your own patterns before making dramatic moves. Dreams about the relationship may be teaching you something your waking mind is not ready to hear.
cards.love_reversed: Reversed in love, The Moon means clarity returning - the illusion about a partner dissolving (for better or for worse), the fear-based patterns finally being named, the truth arriving in a way that allows conscious choice.
cards.career_upright: Upright in career, The Moon signals a period of uncertainty, hidden dynamics in the workplace, or a creative project still gestating in your subconscious. Favors careers in psychology, dream work, art, music, film, mysticism. Intuition is your primary data source right now.
cards.career_reversed: Reversed in career, The Moon can mean hidden workplace politics finally being named, or your own self-deception about a role ending. Clarity returning is often uncomfortable before it is freeing.
cards.spiritual: Spiritually, The Moon is the deep subconscious, the ancestral, the shadow, the womb-time. Her practice is dream journaling, moon cycle tracking, meditation in darkness, and radical honesty about what the body knows that the mind has not admitted.
cards.numerology_note: The Moon is number 18, which reduces to 9 - completion, release, the wise elder. The moon teaches through the long cycle of showing and hiding. If The Moon appears and your Personal Year is a 9 or 18, the year is about completing cycles that have been winding for decades.
cards.astrology_note: The Moon is ruled by Pisces, the mutable water sign of dreams, mysticism, boundaries, and the dissolving of the separate self. Strong Pisces placements carry this dreamwalker energy. Your natal Moon in the 12th house is a double Moon invitation.
cards.forecast_2026: In 2026, The Moon is appearing for those doing deep subconscious work before 2027's shift. The shift is cleaner for those who have faced what the moon has been showing them in dreams for years. If The Moon is appearing for you, the dream journal is the homework.
cards.archetype: The Moon is the Dreamer, the Mystic, the Shadow Guide, the Subconscious itself. She is the archetype of the path that winds because the soul needed the winding.
cards.yes_no: Uncertain - wait for the fog to lift.
cards.affirmation: I trust the winding path. The moon teaches me in her own time.

cards.name: The Sun
cards.symbols: Large radiant sun with a face, naked child riding a white horse, red banner, four giant sunflowers, gray stone wall, clear blue sky
cards.symbolism_deep: A huge radiant sun dominates the sky, with a calm human face at its center. Beneath it, a naked child rides a white horse bareback, holding a red banner in one hand. Behind them, four tall sunflowers bloom over a gray stone wall. The sky is crystalline blue. The child is undefended, unarmed, uncovered - and utterly safe. This is the card of joy after the winding moon, clarity after the tower, innocence restored at a higher level than before.
cards.upright_tag: Joy, success, vitality, clarity, celebration, child-like wonder
cards.reversed_tag: Temporary sadness, dimmed optimism, delayed success, overconfidence
cards.upright_deep: The Sun upright is the card of full radiance. The obstacles have cleared. The joy is not performed; it is genuine. The child on the horse rides without fear because the path has opened and the sun is full. When The Sun appears, the success is real, the recognition is warranted, the happiness is allowed. Stop bracing. Let it be good.
cards.reversed_deep: Reversed, The Sun is only a slight dimming. Success is still coming but delayed. Joy is temporarily clouded. Or you may be shining so brightly outward that you have forgotten to also shine inward. The medicine is not more effort; it is rest and quiet self-celebration.
cards.love_upright: In love, The Sun upright is the best card in the deck. Joyful partnership, playful intimacy, a proposal, a pregnancy, a wedding, a reconciliation that glows. For singles, it can signal a partner arriving who feels like warmth itself. Summers of love are here.
cards.love_reversed: Reversed in love, The Sun can signal temporary overcast. The relationship is good but in a low season. Or you are hiding joy behind a practiced stoicism. Let yourself be happy where you are happy.
cards.career_upright: Upright in career, The Sun is success, recognition, a promotion, a launch, a win. Favors careers in performance, teaching, children, creative arts. It also signals that the work you have been doing is finally visible to others in the way you knew it deserved to be seen.
cards.career_reversed: Reversed in career, The Sun signals delayed recognition or a success you are downplaying. Own the win. The sun does not apologize for shining.
cards.spiritual: Spiritually, The Sun is enlightenment in its embodied, joyful form - not the cave-dwelling monk version, but the child-on-a-horse-under-the-sun version. The light is not hidden. The joy is not fake. You have returned to innocence through the mountain, not by avoiding the mountain.
cards.numerology_note: The Sun is number 19, which reduces to 1 (via 10) - a new beginning at the highest vibration. If The Sun appears and your Personal Year is a 1 or 19, the year is radiantly yours. Claim the spotlight.
cards.astrology_note: The Sun is ruled by the Sun itself - the ego, the self, the core vitality. Your natal Sun sign is your solar identity. When The Sun card appears, your natal Sun's qualities are being amplified.
cards.forecast_2026: In 2026, The Sun is appearing for those being invited to embody full joy before 2027's next chapter. The shift is not austere; it is luminous. If The Sun is appearing for you, celebrate visibly. Your radiance lights paths for others.
cards.archetype: The Sun is the Eternal Child, the Divine Joy, the Radiant Self. It is the archetype of innocence-through-experience - the wisdom that remembers how to play.
cards.yes_no: Yes - a clear and joyful yes.
cards.affirmation: I shine without apology. My joy is real. My success is welcome.

cards.name: Judgement
cards.symbols: Archangel Gabriel blowing a golden trumpet, banner with red cross, naked figures rising from gray coffins with arms upraised, mountains in distance, clouds
cards.symbolism_deep: Archangel Gabriel descends from the clouds, blowing a long golden trumpet from which a banner of the red cross flies. Below, gray coffins open in the water and naked figures rise with their arms raised in welcome. A mountain range stretches behind them. This is the call, the awakening, the moment the soul is invited to rise into a higher version of itself. The coffins are not death; they are the old identities that had already died. The call is what awakens you to rise.
cards.upright_tag: Awakening, rebirth, calling, inner judgement, absolution
cards.reversed_tag: Self-doubt, ignoring the call, harsh self-judgement, resistance to evolution
cards.upright_deep: Judgement upright is the call to rise. Something is waking you up - a book, a song, a person, a near-death, a long-dormant dream. The coffin-identities you have been carrying (victim, small, quiet, wounded) are ready to be left behind. The trumpet is sounding. You hear it or you do not. When you hear it, the rising is not difficult. It is inevitable.
cards.reversed_deep: Reversed, Judgement warns of the call being ignored - the book unopened, the voice unheeded, the inner knowing silenced by the busier life. Or it can mean harsh self-judgement: not forgiveness, but flagellation. The medicine is to answer the call gently, with compassion for the self that resisted.
cards.love_upright: In love, Judgement upright signals a reunion, a reconciliation, a relationship reborn at a higher level. The trumpet call often brings back a soul connection in a new form. For couples, it can mean a vow renewed after profound transformation.
cards.love_reversed: Reversed in love, Judgement can mean refusing to forgive (self or other), holding the old identity of the relationship past its expiration, or ignoring a call to honor the connection properly.
cards.career_upright: Upright in career, Judgement is the calling card. The work you are actually here to do. Not the job - the vocation. It favors careers in healing, teaching, spirituality, activism, and any work that feels like a response to a deeper call rather than a career-building move.
cards.career_reversed: Reversed in career, Judgement can mean a calling you keep postponing, or a career built around ignoring the inner voice. The trumpet is patient but persistent.
cards.spiritual: Spiritually, Judgement is the awakening proper - the moment the soul remembers why it came. Every spiritual tradition has its trumpet. The second coming, the Bodhisattva vow, the initiation, the great awakening. Your version is calling you personally, by name.
cards.numerology_note: Judgement is number 20, which reduces to 2 (via 10) - partnership, balance, listening. The awakening is relational; it is a response to a call. If Judgement appears and your Personal Year is a 2 or 20, the year is about answering the call you have been hearing.
cards.astrology_note: Judgement is ruled by Pluto, the planet of deep transformation, death and rebirth, the underworld, ruthless truth. Pluto transits often coincide with Judgement moments - the old self cannot be saved; the new self is the rising.
cards.forecast_2026: In 2026, Judgement is appearing for those hearing their call clearly for the first time in years. The 2027 shift is the collective Judgement moment. If Judgement is appearing for you, the call is real. Rise.
cards.archetype: Judgement is the Awakened One, the Called, the Reborn. It is the archetype of the soul answering the trumpet and rising from the coffin of the old identity.
cards.affirmation: I hear the call. I rise into who I am becoming.

cards.name: The World
cards.symbols: Dancing figure surrounded by a laurel wreath (vesica piscis), two wands in hands, four creatures at corners (man, eagle, lion, bull), purple sash
cards.symbolism_deep: A dancing figure, wrapped in a purple sash, stands inside a laurel wreath shaped like a vesica piscis (the sacred almond shape of union). In each hand, a short wand. At the four corners, the same four living creatures from The Wheel of Fortune: man, eagle, lion, bull - the fixed zodiac signs and the four elements. This is the card of completion, wholeness, the circle closed, the journey through the other 21 cards integrated. The dancer is not in a hurry. The dance is the destination.
cards.upright_tag: Completion, wholeness, integration, accomplishment, world travel
cards.reversed_tag: Incomplete, loose ends, delay, lack of closure, almost-there
cards.upright_deep: The World upright is the card of completion. Something large is finishing - a degree, a chapter, a version of yourself, a relationship, a business cycle. The dancer in the wreath has arrived. The four corners are integrated - fire, water, earth and air are all present, and so are all four fixed signs of the zodiac. This is not the end of life; it is the end of one soul chapter. Celebrate. Integrate. The next chapter begins from a wholly new place.
cards.reversed_deep: Reversed, The World warns of an almost-complete journey - the last 5 percent undone, the loose ends unfinished, the chapter you keep extending past its proper close. The medicine is to honor the actual completion rather than to postpone it because the next chapter feels uncertain.
cards.love_upright: In love, The World upright is the fulfilling partnership, the marriage that has arrived at maturity, the completion of a relational chapter with wholeness. For singles, it can mean the completion of a single chapter and readiness for the next relationship from full rather than from lack.
cards.love_reversed: Reversed in love, The World can mean a relationship close to completion but held open, a divorce that should be finalized but is not, or a partnership that is 95 percent ready for the next level and waiting on the last 5 percent.
cards.career_upright: Upright in career, The World is graduation, the book finished, the company sold, the major project completed. It often coincides with international success, world travel, a career that crosses borders. The work is whole.
cards.career_reversed: Reversed in career, The World means a project at 90 percent that you cannot finish, or a success not fully claimed. Complete it. Close it. The next door only opens when this one is fully shut.
cards.spiritual: Spiritually, The World is enlightenment in its embodied form - not the transcendent kind but the integrated kind. You have gone through the 22 cards, you have met the Fool and the Magician and the Hermit and the Devil and the Star, and now you dance. The dance is the path. The path is the dance.
cards.numerology_note: The World is number 21, which reduces to 3 - creative expression, trinity, communion. Completion as celebration. If The World appears and your Personal Year is a 3 or 21, the year is about finishing with joy what you started long ago.
cards.astrology_note: The World is ruled by Saturn, the planet of structure, time, karma and completion. Saturn returns (at 28-30 and 56-60) often coincide with World moments - the completion of a full karmic cycle. Your natal Saturn shows where you are most asked to complete before moving on.
cards.forecast_2026: In 2026, The World is appearing for those completing the large chapter of 2023-2025 before 2027 opens. The consciousness shift rewards those who have fully closed what needs closing. If The World is appearing, the completion is at hand. Honor it with ceremony.
cards.archetype: The World is the Integrated One, the Whole Soul, the Dancer in the Wreath. It is the archetype of completion as celebration and of the circle returning to the Fool - wiser, lighter, ready for the next spiral.
cards.affirmation: I am whole. The journey is complete. I dance into the next spiral.
